title: STAT+: CMS halts enrollment in Elevance’s Medicare Advantage plans, citing years of misconduct
summary: CMS said Elevance had "substantial and persistent noncompliance" with requirements to submit information about its customers.

summary: The health insurer paid higher prices for hospital care than some rivals in the ACA insurance market.

title: Ascendis wins FDA approval of dwarfism drug

title: UniQure says FDA wants another study of Huntington’s gene therapy
summary: Agency staff "strongly recommended" a sham surgery-controlled trial be conducted before an approval filing, a stance one analyst called a "worst case scenario" for UniQure.

title: Roche pill succeeds in another MS study, but approval questions linger
summary: Liver-related side effects have led some analysts to question fenebrutinib’s prospects, even though the drug has now cleared Phase 3 studies in multiple forms of the disease.

title: Candid, in a reverse merger with RallyBio, to go public
summary: Candid, which is making bispecifics for immune diseases, has also raised $505 million in a private financing for the newly formed company.

title: Safety concerns spur Aardvark to halt key Prader-Willi drug trial

summary: UniQure said plans to seek approval for its Huntington’s disease treatment with the FDA remain blocked.

summary: Reduce risk, cut costs and scale smarter by closing open steps in cell therapy manufacturing.

title: STAT+: Federal Medicaid audit finds massive overpayment for autism therapy in Colorado
summary: HHS's OIG uncovered $285.2 million in improper and potentially improper payments to clinicians of an autism therapy called applied behavior analysis, it said.

summary: It’s time to rethink punitive policies around cannabis use by people who are pregnant.

title: STAT+: Trump’s drive to get Europe to pay more for drugs creates uncertainty for countries, patients
summary: President Trump's demand that wealthy countries in Europe spend more on drugs so the U.S. can pay less has introduced uncertainty across the continent.

title: Atrium spins out of Avidity, aiming to target rare heart diseases with RNA
summary: Four months after Avidity’s $12 billion buyout, Atrium debuted with $270 million in cash and plans to pick up its predecessor’s work in cardiovascular disease.

summary: In issuing a positive recommendation, the EMA has diverged from the FDA, which has set higher approval standards for Moderna’s vaccine in the U.S.

title: Generate caps a strong month for biotech IPOs with $400M offering

title: FDA Grants Second Approval under the National Priority Voucher Pilot Program
